== Notes for [[Item:Q87957|Q87957]] ==

'''Lucius Julius Caesar I''', a Tribunus Militum (about 250 BCE). He belonged to an ancient Roman family that traced its descent from Aeneas of Troy, son of the goddess Venus, who fled that city when it fell. Aeneas' son Ascanius, called Iulus, was supposed to have been the ancestor of Romulus and Remus, founders of Rome. The name "Caesar" means a hairy head or an elephant.

Son of Numerius Julius Caesar and father to

# Sextus Julius Caesar I.

* Lucius was a great-grandson to Lucius Julius Libo.

== Sources ==

* http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucius_Julius_Caesar#Lucius_Julius_Caesar_I

--------------------
ID: I62063

Name: Lucius Julius Caesar of Rome

Given Name: Lucius Julius

Surname: Caesar of Rome

Sex: M

_UID: D5975C7E19154743A095DCF1657E3D1D3D60

Change Date: 26 Nov 2005

Death: Y

Father: Lucius Julius Caesar of Rome

Marriage 1 Spouse Unknown

Children

Numerius Julius Caesar

Forrás / Source:

http://wc.rootsweb.ancestry.com/cgi-bin/igm.cgi?op=GET&db=jdp-fam&id=I62063

Lucius Libo*? of Rome

Consul 267 BC

Father

Lucius Julius Libo of Rome

Children

Numerius Julius Caesar of Rome

----------
https://homepages.rpi.edu/~holmes/Hobbies/Genealogy2/ps32/ps32_026.htm

----------

SOURCE NOTES:
http://www.american-pictures.com/genealogy/persons/per09766.htm#0

RESEARCH NOTES:
of Rome; Consul 267 BC

CONSUL WITH MARCUS ATTILUS REGULUS


wtf 4:565

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
